title: Economy is shown through labour chains
source_observation: The epic names specialised occupations while archaeology preserves production and exchange evidence
risk: Markets could become decorative displays of finished luxury goods
decision: Show extraction making transport valuation sale repair and waste for every major commodity
rationale: Makes wealth materially and socially legible
required_review: Economic history; craft archaeology; production design; screenplay

title: Performers are skilled workers
source_observation: Chapter 2 presents a broad disciplined curriculum for performing women
risk: Madhavi's world could be reduced to beauty and erotic spectacle
decision: Show training rehearsal musicians maintenance patron negotiation and household economics alongside artistic authority
required_review: Classical Tamil; performance history; gender history; choreography

title: No Roman colonial port framing
source_observation: Imported ceramics and coins occur at Tamil coastal and inland sites
risk: Familiar visual shorthand may turn Puhar into a Roman settlement or make foreigners the source of local prosperity
decision: Represent imported objects within Tamil-led multi-stage exchange and use foreign residents only where evidence supports them
required_review: Indian Ocean archaeology; classical studies; casting; publicity

title: No medieval guild institutions in early Puhar
source_observation: Early corporate references are limited while richly documented guild systems belong mainly to later centuries
risk: Guild banners armies titles and charters could be imported from medieval inscriptions
decision: Show collective merchant practice through reputation witnesses storage and coordinated travel without later institutional forms
required_review: Epigraphy; economic history; screenplay; production design

title: Mixed forms of exchange
source_observation: Coins weights grain gifts services alms and valuable objects coexist in the evidence
risk: A coin-only marketplace would impose a modern monetised economy
decision: Use multiple forms of value transfer and approve no exact conversion rate or universal denomination before review
rationale: Creates plausible exchange while retaining uncertainty

title: Writing on objects requires epigraphic control
source_observation: Tamil-Brahmi occurs on portable objects but its function varies
risk: Decorative pseudo-script or convenient ownership labels would manufacture evidence
decision: Every inscription prop must use reviewed text purpose placement and palaeography
rationale: Protects textual integrity and public trust
required_review: Tamil-Brahmi epigraphy; prop design; art department

title: Craft technique does not code ethnicity
source_observation: Connected craft communities used varied techniques across multiple sites
risk: Costume and casting could turn one technology into an ethnic stereotype
decision: Differentiate workers through documented practice and biography rather than assigning ethnicity from product style
required_review: Craft archaeology; anthropology; casting; sensitivity review

title: Merchant wealth includes distributed risk
source_observation: Voyages depend on crews labour suppliers and families while wrecks distribute loss unevenly
risk: Merchants could appear as isolated heroic wealth creators
decision: Show pilots loaders packers repairers dependants and competing claims around every major voyage or cargo loss
rationale: Connects maritime spectacle to labour and vulnerability

title: Poverty is connected to disrupted livelihood
source_observation: The epic links hunger with injury begging famine and failed access
risk: Poor characters could appear as a timeless anonymous class
decision: Use recurring households and visible loss of work tools mobility and market access before relief scenes
required_review: Social history; disability review; famine studies; screenplay
